```json
  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Schrader cores left in place during evacuation",
    "Evacuation cannot achieve deep vacuum with cores installed due to trapped volume and restricted flow",
    "Residual moisture and non-condensables remain in system",
    "Accelerates acid formation, compressor damage, and refrigerant degradation",
    "Non-compliant with OEM brazing and evacuation procedures"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recover refrigerant per EPA 608",
    "Remove Schrader cores and install core removal tool or vacuum-rated valves",
    "Perform triple evacuation or deep vacuum to specified microns per OEM",
    "Replace Schrader cores and dehydrator/drier if indicated",
    "Pressure test, evacuate, and charge per manufacturer specs"
  ],
  "rationale": "Leaving Schrader cores in during evacuation prevents the system from being properly evacuated. The core and internal volume trap air and moisture, blocking the deep vacuum required to remove non-condensables and moisture. This is a known installation/commissioning defect that leads to system contamination, acid formation, and compressor failure. Because the asset is in service, the defect is latent and the system is operating with compromised refrigerant chemistry, making this a high-risk non-compliance.",
  "references": [
    "ACCA",
    "EPA 608",
    "OEM brazing and evacuation specifications"
  ]
}
```
